title: oxalis dimidiata
intro: Oxalis dimidiata, also known as the split-root wood sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family, characterized by its distinctive ability to grow two separate roots from a single stem. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Oxalis dimidiata are said to include the treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Oxalis dimidiata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies due to its purported health benefits.
